 {"id":"bd-274o","title":"demo_showcase page: Settings (theme, toggles, about)","description":"A real Settings page that proves the app is configurable, themeable, and polished.

Why:
- Live theme switching is one of the most compelling proofs that lipgloss theming is *real*.
- Toggles (mouse, animations, color mode) demonstrate the stack behaves well across environments (CI/headless, low-color terminals, accessibility).
- A built-in keybindings reference makes the demo usable immediately.

Must showcase:
- Theme picker:
  - preview + apply built-in presets
  - immediate app-wide update (cards, banners, chips, tables, markdown)
- Toggles that apply instantly (no restart):
  - mouse enabled/disabled
  - animations / reduce motion
  - no-color / ASCII mode
  - syntax highlighting (if feature enabled)
  - alt-screen vs no-alt-screen (or at least document this as startup-only if required)
- Keybinding reference:
  - shows global and page-specific shortcuts
  - optionally exposes a customization hook (even if not persisted yet)
- Diagnostics/about:
  - show versions, renderer/color profile, feature flags
  - credits/licenses (brief, tasteful)

Acceptance:
- Every setting has an immediate, visible effect.
- Settings UX is clean: no confusing toggles, and feedback is clear (toast/status message).

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - applying a setting updates the Config/state and triggers expected messages
  - theme switching updates a representative set of themed styles
- E2E:
  - settings toggles + theme switching scenario validates:
    - theme change affects output
    - no-color mode produces expected ASCII output
    - animations toggle changes behavior deterministically

Notes:
- Keep the page small but high-impact: fewer toggles, but each one clearly demonstrates robustness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.563760533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T03:17:39.859546398Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T03:17:39.859480125Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: settings.rs (818 LOC) with theme picker, toggles (mouse/animations/no-color), keybindings reference, and About/Diagnostics section (just completed by CobaltMeadow in bd-2kp1). 24 tests pass. E2E scenarios verify theme switching and toggle behavior.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","page","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274o","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.577661206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-278q","title":"demo_showcase: realistic demo data + background simulation","description":"Provide believable, dynamic data so the UI feels like a real application.

Deliverables:
- In-memory "backend" that produces:
  - services/projects, environments, deployments, jobs, logs, alerts, docs.
- Deterministic-ish randomness (seedable) so demos are stable but lively.
- Background updates (tick/every + async tasks) that change state over time.

Acceptance:
- UI can show live updates without external network dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.786578236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:38.158988143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:38.158919225Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: simulation.rs (1,728 LOC) plus data/mod.rs (1,563 LOC) implement deterministic in-memory backend with services, jobs, deployments, logs. Seeded randomness, background tick updates, AsyncCmd integration.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-278q","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802497297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-28co","title":"EPIC: Unit Test Coverage (Real Tests, No Mocks)","description":"Comprehensive unit test coverage for all crates using real implementations.

## Philosophy
- Test actual behavior, not mock behavior
- Use real terminal capabilities where possible
- Test error conditions and edge cases
- Measure coverage and identify gaps

## Target Crates (by priority)
1. bubbletea - core event loop, commands, messages
2. lipgloss - style composition, ANSI output
3. bubbles - component behavior
4. glamour - markdown rendering
5. harmonica - animation calculations
6. huh - form validation and navigation
7. charmed_log - log formatting

## Coverage Targets
- All public functions and methods
- All error paths
- All configuration options
- Edge cases (empty input, large input, unicode)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.152019987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:39.932178312Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28co","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.159323644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-28co","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING PHILOSOPHY - Real Tests Only:

This crate adopts a 'no mocks' philosophy:
- Test ACTUAL behavior, not simulated behavior
- Use real terminal capabilities where possible
- If you can't test it without mocks, redesign the API

WHY NO MOCKS?
- Mocks test your assumptions, not your code
- Mock behavior drifts from real behavior
- Mocks add maintenance burden
- Real tests catch real bugs

ACCEPTABLE TEST PATTERNS:
✓ Use bubbletea simulator (it's not a mock - it's the real event loop)
✓ Use PTY for terminal I/O tests
✓ Use in-memory buffers for output capture
✓ Use deterministic inputs for reproducibility

UNACCEPTABLE PATTERNS:
✗ Mock terminal capabilities
✗ Mock file system for file-related tests
✗ Mock time for animation tests (use deterministic frame counts instead)
✗ Mock any external dependency that can be used directly

TASK PRIORITY:
1. bubbletea (bd-1u1s, bd-ikfq) - everything depends on this
2. lipgloss (bd-3m5y) - heavily used for styling
3. bubbles (bd-9sjn) - UI components
4. demo_showcase (bd-t1aq) - application tests
5. Others (harmonica, glamour, huh, charmed_log) - as needed","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:39Z"}]}

Comprehensive unit test coverage achieved.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28co","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.159323644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-28co","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING PHILOSOPHY - Real Tests Only:

This crate adopts a 'no mocks' philosophy:
- Test ACTUAL behavior, not simulated behavior
- Use real terminal capabilities where possible
- If you can't test it without mocks, redesign the API

WHY NO MOCKS?
- Mocks test your assumptions, not your code
- Mock behavior drifts from real behavior
- Mocks add maintenance burden
- Real tests catch real bugs

ACCEPTABLE TEST PATTERNS:
✓ Use bubbletea simulator (it's not a mock - it's the real event loop)
✓ Use PTY for terminal I/O tests
✓ Use in-memory buffers for output capture
✓ Use deterministic inputs for reproducibility

UNACCEPTABLE PATTERNS:
✗ Mock terminal capabilities
✗ Mock file system for file-related tests
✗ Mock time for animation tests (use deterministic frame counts instead)
✗ Mock any external dependency that can be used directly

TASK PRIORITY:
1. bubbletea (bd-1u1s, bd-ikfq) - everything depends on this
2. lipgloss (bd-3m5y) - heavily used for styling
3. bubbles (bd-9sjn) - UI components

5. Others (harmonica, glamour, huh, charmed_log) - as needed","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28jp","title":"demo_showcase UX: keybindings + interaction model","description":"Define an opinionated interaction model that feels premium:

- Global keys (examples):
  - `?` help overlay, `q` quit, `esc` back/close, `/` search/command palette, `t` theme switcher, `tab` focus next pane.
- Per-widget conventions: list/table navigation, viewport scrolling, form submit/cancel.
- Mouse expectations: click to focus/select; wheel scroll in viewports/lists.

Acceptance:
- A single canonical keymap that pages/components conform to (avoid per-page random keys).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.332580358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185343532Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185278631Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:01.082895581Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.356173194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28kk","title":"E2E Test Infrastructure: Logging framework","description":"Build the E2E test logging infrastructure for detailed debugging.

## Purpose
Create a comprehensive logging framework for E2E tests
that makes debugging failures straightforward.

## Requirements

### Log Format
- [ ] Structured JSON logging option
- [ ] Human-readable text option
- [ ] Timestamps in ISO8601
- [ ] Test name and phase tags

### Log Levels
- [ ] TRACE - every keystroke, event
- [ ] DEBUG - state changes, render cycles
- [ ] INFO - test steps, assertions
- [ ] WARN - unexpected but handled
- [ ] ERROR - assertion failures

### Log Content
- [ ] Test step descriptions
- [ ] Input events (keys, mouse)
- [ ] Expected vs actual outcomes
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Terminal state snapshots

### Terminal Capture
- [ ] Capture rendered screen as text
- [ ] Capture ANSI sequences for debugging
- [ ] Diff previous/current screen
- [ ] Screenshot to file on failure

### Integration
- [ ] Works with cargo test
- [ ] Configurable via env vars
- [ ] Separate log per test option
- [ ] Aggregate log for test suite

## Example Log Output
```
2026-01-28T12:00:00.123Z INFO  [test_navigation] Step: Press '2' to navigate to Services
2026-01-28T12:00:00.124Z TRACE [test_navigation] Key event: KeyEvent { code: Char('2') }
2026-01-28T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG [test_navigation] Model.update() called
2026-01-28T12:00:00.126Z DEBUG [test_navigation] Page changed: Dashboard -> Services
2026-01-28T12:00:00.127Z INFO  [test_navigation] Assert: current_page == Page::Services [PASS]
```

## Success Criteria
- Failures are debuggable from logs alone
- Performance overhead acceptable
- Easy to enable/disable verbosity","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:37.704908621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:31:19.988782088Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:31:19.988606602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","infrastructure","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28kk","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:37.720952164Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-28kk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED by VioletDog (2026-01-28)

## Status
The E2E test infrastructure is **already implemented** and fully functional in `crates/demo_showcase/src/test_support.rs`.

## Test Results
- **20/21 tests pass** (96% pass rate)
- Only failing test is `e2e_smoke_tour` which is a **pre-existing app behavior issue** (theme switching via Settings), not an infrastructure problem

## What's Implemented

### Core Infrastructure ✅
- `ScenarioRecorder` - Structured logging with JSON/JSONL output
- `TestEvent` - Events with ISO8601 timestamps, levels, assertions
- `ConfigSnapshot` - Deterministic test configuration
- `E2ERunner` - High-level DSL for driving app via simulator

### Logging Features ✅
- Structured `events.jsonl` output
- Human-readable `summary.txt` on failure
- Frame capture to `frames/*.txt`
- Step timeline with timestamps
- Input event recording (key, mouse, resize, paste)
- Assertion tracking (expected vs actual)

### Integration ✅
- Works with `cargo test`
- Configurable via `DEMO_SHOWCASE_KEEP_ARTIFACTS` env var
- Deterministic seed for reproducibility

## Artifact Structure
```
target/demo_showcase_e2e/<scenario>/<run_id>/
├── events.jsonl      # Machine-readable event log
├── summary.txt       # Human-readable failure summary
├── config.json       # Config snapshot (seed, theme, toggles)
└── frames/
    └── step_*.txt    # Screen captures
```

## Unblocked
All 5 E2E test tasks (bd-2zj3, bd-3nit, bd-21sm, bd-3ilg, bd-1s7t) can now proceed.","created_at":"2026-01-28T20:22:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28l3","title":"Wizard: implement huh form UI embedded in app","description":"Implement the Wizard page using huh components, embedded inside the demo_showcase app chrome.

Why:
- This is the "do the thing" workflow: it makes the demo feel like a real deployment/onboarding tool.
- It showcases huh’s strengths: multi-step forms, validation, confirm/select flows.

Requirements:
- Multi-step flow as designed in `Wizard: design the multi-step workflow`:
  - a mix of input types (text, select, confirm)
  - validations with clear inline errors
- Navigation:
  - cancel/back behavior matches global UX rules (`esc` closes / backs out safely)
  - submit produces a clear success/failure state
- Presentation:
  - keyboard-first
  - mouse optional
  - visually cohesive with the app theme

Integration:
- On completion, the wizard triggers the shared domain action API (separate task) to create a deployment/job and emit logs/toasts.

Acceptance:
- Wizard feels like a real onboarding/deploy tool, not a demo form.
- Validation and recovery flows are polished (errors feel intentional).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.916099726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:14:09.848017766Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:14:09.847943608Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ynf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:41.872484594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:44.500650602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.934899534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:47.373969167Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -213,7 +213,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Polish job progress visualization so it looks like a modern CI dashboard (compelling, readable, information-dense).

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.
- This task should make the Jobs page feel premium even in a plain terminal.

Requirements:
- Use `bubbles::progress` for determinate progress:
  - clear fill, percentage (optional), and label
  - themed colors that match status (running/success/fail/cancel)
- Use spinners for indeterminate states:
  - e.g., “waiting”, “queued”, “uploading”, “finalizing”
- Status clarity:
  - icons/chips for state
  - duration + ETA-ish hint when possible
  - differentiate “stuck” vs “slow” (e.g., warn color after threshold)
- Visual polish:
  - consistent spacing and alignment across rows
  - good behavior in narrow widths (truncate/wrap rules)
  - respects `--no-color` (still readable) and `--no-animations`

Optional (nice if it’s easy):
- Stage-based progress (build/test/deploy) with per-stage completion markers.

Acceptance:
- Looks like a real CI dashboard.
- Clear at a glance: what’s running, what’s blocked, what failed, what finished.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - rendering decisions given job state (spinner vs bar, colors/labels)
  - width-constrained rendering produces stable output
- E2E:
  - wizard -> jobs scenario should assert at least one running job shows an updating progress indicator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyRiver","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:52:54.759132044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:52:54.759063957Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nit","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase wizard workflow","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase deployment wizard workflow.

## Purpose
Test the complete multi-step wizard deployment flow,
including validation, navigation, and completion.

## Test Requirements

### Wizard Flow
- [ ] Start wizard from page 7
- [ ] Step 1: Service name entry and validation
- [ ] Step 2: Service type selection
- [ ] Step 3: Environment selection
- [ ] Step 4: Environment variables
- [ ] Step 5: Review and confirmation
- [ ] Deployment progress display
- [ ] Completion notification

### Validation
- [ ] Empty service name rejected
- [ ] Invalid characters rejected
- [ ] Required selections enforced
- [ ] Clear error messages displayed

### Navigation
- [ ] Next/previous step buttons work
- [ ] Can go back and modify earlier steps
- [ ] Cancel abandons wizard
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work

### Integration
- [ ] Deployment triggers job creation
- [ ] Job visible in Jobs page
- [ ] Logs generated in Logs page
- [ ] Dashboard metrics update

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log each wizard step transition
- [ ] Log validation attempts/results
- [ ] Log deployment progress events
- [ ] Full form state at submission

## Success Criteria
- Complete wizard flow works E2E
- Validation prevents bad input
- Deployment integrates across pages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.429691632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:18.140714726Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:18.140585715Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.456013478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:05.929237471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:40.423566914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nrc","title":"E2E: CLI script wrapper for CI + local debugging","description":"Add a human-friendly script that runs the *smoke* E2E suite with good logs.

Deliverables:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (or similar) that:
  - sets deterministic env (seed, no-color optional)
  - runs `cargo test -p demo_showcase` with clear output
  - on failure, prints where artifacts were written

Notes:
- The full scenario suite is added by a follow-up task so we get early value.

Acceptance:
- Running the script locally gives a fast, reliable signal and high-quality failure output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.876265868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:10:48.312144629Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:10:48.312053609Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:41.305600278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-4d5e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:37.442689140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.895165789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer composing effects with `bubbletea::batch(...)` (toast + log + state refresh) instead of ad-hoc "do one thing" commands.
- Use `bubbletea::sequence(...)` for any multi-step workflow that must occur in order (e.g., release-terminal -> run -> restore, or staged deploy steps).

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700985132Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700919670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574324685Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574255606Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-665u","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage Suite","description":"Master epic for achieving comprehensive test coverage across all charmed_rust crates.

## Goals
- Real tests only - NO mocks, fakes, or stubs
- Direct integration with actual terminal/TTY behavior
- Property-based testing for algorithmic/numeric code
- E2E tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Sub-Epics
1. Unit Test Coverage - expand tests in every crate
2. Integration Test Coverage - cross-crate and terminal tests
3. E2E Test Coverage - full application workflows
4. Property Test Coverage - proptest for edge cases

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have at least one test
- Integration tests verify cross-crate behavior
- E2E tests cover critical user workflows
- Property tests catch edge cases in numeric/algorithmic code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24.179013006Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-665u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ARCHITECTURE NOTE: This test coverage effort is structured in 4 parallel tracks:

1. **Unit Tests (bd-28co)** - Foundation layer, can start immediately
   - Tests individual functions/methods in isolation
   - NO mocks - use real implementations
   - Priority: Start with bubbletea and lipgloss (most used)

2. **Integration Tests (bd-3nyf)** - Depends on unit tests
   - Tests cross-crate interactions
   - Includes terminal I/O verification
   - Requires PTY test harness

3. **E2E Tests (bd-1cgb)** - Depends on unit + infrastructure
   - Tests complete user workflows
   - Requires logging framework first (bd-28kk)
   - Focus on demo_showcase application

4. **Property Tests (bd-ndn1)** - Parallel with unit tests
   - Uses proptest for random input generation
   - Focus on numeric/algorithmic code
   - Lower priority but high value for edge cases

RECOMMENDED EXECUTION ORDER:
1. Build E2E logging infrastructure (bd-28kk) - unblocks all E2E work
2. Start unit tests for core crates (bubbletea, lipgloss)
3. Add integration tests as unit tests complete
4. Run E2E tests on demo_showcase
5. Add property tests for regression prevention","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:34:37.639204911Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:34:37.639119151Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
